title: Upgrade the prior hazard idea into a censored time-to-next-pit AFT model
summary: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem: rows before the next pit have an observed remaining-laps label, while rows with no later pit in the race are right-censored.
rationale: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem: rows before the next pit have an observed remaining-laps label, while rows with no later pit in the race are right-censored. AFT survival models are built for exactly this setting and may extract more signal than direct binary next-lap classification when the decision surface is really a shrinking pit-window.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race sequence, compute remaining laps until next pit stop; if no future pit exists, mark the label as right-censored at laps_remaining_to_flag. Train an XGBoost `survival:aft` model on existing features plus a few regime features, then convert predicted survival time into `P(pit in next lap)` using a calibrated mapping from predicted remaining-life to empirical next-lap event frequency.
expected_effect: Could improve CV by capturing the full pit-window trajectory instead of only the last-step boundary; also gives a second model family with genuinely different ranking behavior for blending.
risk: Need careful label construction and OOF conversion from survival output to next-lap probability; a poor mapping can erase the benefit even if the survival fit is strong.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
